core_behavior: The system must record and store user interactions with the system, including but not limited to: Login and logout events, Page views and navigation, Form submissions and data modifications, Search queries and results, Error messages and exceptions.
The system will log user activity at the individual user level, allowing for the tracking of specific user behavior over time.
inputs: Event type (e.g., login, page view, form submission)
Relevant event data (e.g., page URL, form data, search query)
User agent information (e.g., browser type, operating system)
outputs: The system will produce a log entry for each user activity event, containing the following information: User identifier, Event type, Event timestamp, Relevant event data, User agent information.
Log entries will be stored in a database or file system, allowing for later retrieval and analysis.
assumptions: The system will use a relational database management system (e.g., MySQL) to store log entries.
Log entries will be stored in a separate table from other system data to improve query performance.
The system will use a standardized logging format (e.g., JSON) to facilitate log analysis and processing.
Log retention will be set to 365 days, after which log entries will be automatically deleted.
failure_modes: Attempt to write log entries to a temporary storage location (e.g., a queue) if the primary log storage location is unavailable.
Raise an error and notify system administrators if log writing fails for an extended period (e.g., 30 minutes).
Continue to operate and process user requests, even if log writing is temporarily unavailable.
edge_cases: Anonymous users: The system will log activity for anonymous users using a generated identifier (e.g., a session ID).
Multiple user agents: The system will log user agent information for each user session, even if the user switches between different devices or browsers.
High-volume activity: The system will implement rate limiting and queueing to prevent log writing from impacting system performance during periods of high user activity.
Log storage capacity: The system will monitor log storage capacity and alert system administrators when the storage location approaches maximum capacity.
security_constraints: Data encryption: Log entries will be encrypted at rest using a secure encryption algorithm (e.g., AES-256).
Access control: Log data will be restricted to authorized system administrators and will require authentication and authorization to access.
Audit logging: The system will maintain a separate audit log to track all access to log data, including queries, modifications, and deletions.
performance_constraints: Log writing latency: The system will ensure that log writing latency does not exceed 100ms, even under high load conditions.
Log query performance: The system will optimize log queries to return results within 500ms, even for large log datasets.
System resource utilization: The system will ensure that log writing and storage do not consume more than 10% of system resources (e.g., CPU, memory, disk space).
